opp.name: Apotex [ANA] Nov'25
opp.next_step: 5/4 - checked in with team, still working through consolidation efforts. Invitations to Horizon Sept event have been extended. 4/15 - spent time with Namish Shah in person at NAPES. Interest in ANA but Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further. Tail spend is ~$100Mn/year. Conversations to resume in 6 weeks (June) when they know better how progress is coming.

items.area: Buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity

items.quote: we're still meeting a few solutions provider just to understand what's out there

items.area: Direct Zycus engagement with Ryan Mitchell (VP Global Procurement)
items.quote: Carla reports to Ryan Mitchell, head of Global Procurement
No, currently we don't have a budget.

metrics.narrative: Namish Shah disclosed Apotex's unmanaged tail spend is 'roughly north of $100,000,000' a year (Avoma Intro Call, 07 Nov 2025) and Zycus proposed 2-3% recurring savings via Anna, but Namish pushed back that this looked low against other providers he'd heard claim 15-20% ('we've met companies who have claimed 20%, 15 to 20% ... that's why I'm a little surprised to see 3 to 4%'). No quantified, buyer-owned business case has been confirmed since; the number itself is a live point of friction, not just an open gap.

champion.narrative: Namish Shah is a developing champion: he has stayed engaged across nine months of email and calls even through the consolidation pause, and on 25 Jun 2026 he personally brought Carla Baptista, a Director, into a Zycus-adjacent event, a real piece of internal advocacy. What is still missing is any evidence he has escalated the deal to Ryan Mitchell (VP) himself, secured budget language internally, or pushed back on the consolidation delay on Zycus's behalf.

competition.narrative: The single strongest and most current competitive threat is not a rival vendor but Apotex's own internal push to consolidate tail spend with existing strategic partners, reconfirmed unresolved by Carla Baptista as recently as 25 Jun 2026. Namish Shah also confirmed on 07 Nov 2025 that Apotex was 'still meeting a few solutions provider' in parallel, with no names disclosed. SAP Ariba is the incumbent S2P system of record ('it's more SAP Ariba for the company', Namish Shah, 07 Nov 2025) but is not itself being evaluated as a tail-spend alternative. The wedge for Zycus is positioning Anna as complementary to, not competing with, the consolidation effort.

identify_pain.narrative: Apotex has ~$100M/year of unmanaged tail spend and wants to move it under control (Namish Shah, 07 Nov 2025: 'right now, we do have unmanaged spend, and we are trying to get some control over that and see if there is any opportunity there'), confirmed by the relevant stakeholder_Business_Problem__c field ('a lot of tailspend and want a tool that could reduce their tailspend by a significant amount'). The pain owner is Namish Shah's procurement-excellence function, though the operational urgency has been secondary to the internal consolidation push since April 2026.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed; the deal has not progressed past exploration. The immediate blocker is more basic: an NDA/CDA that Zycus first offered on 07 Nov 2025 ('we could put a CDA in place when you're ready') has still not been signed as of 26 Jun 2026, when the deal owner was again following up with Namish Shah and Carla Baptista to get it executed. Until that NDA is signed, formal tail-spend data sharing and POC scoping (let alone MSA/SOW discussion) cannot start.

economic_buyer.narrative: Carla Baptista (Director, Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (VP Global Procurement, Carla's manager) are named in SF Next_Step_History__c (16 Apr 2026) as the leadership pausing the tail-spend vendor evaluation to consolidate spend with strategic partners first. Carla moved from being referenced secondhand to directly engaging: she attended a Toronto procurement roundtable with Namish Shah on 25 Jun 2026 and asked detailed questions (Task, 26 Jun 2026). Ryan Mitchell has still never been directly engaged by Zycus, so budget sign-off authority remains unconfirmed.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps the process: an informal exploration led solely by Namish Shah (Manager level) through late 2025 and early 2026, gated in April 2026 by a leadership-driven decision (Carla Baptista and Ryan Mitchell) to consolidate tail spend with existing strategic partners before any new-vendor evaluation resumes. As of the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto roundtable that gate was confirmed still open ('nothing had changed much'). No formal approval chain, RFP process, or committee structure has been shared beyond this.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria have been documented. Namish Shah's own words on 07 Nov 2025 frame the process as still informal: 'we have identified an opportunity, next step is to meet some of the potential suppliers who can provide solutions for us, and then we evaluate what is fit for us as an organization.' Security/data residency (healthcare, critical) and SAP Ariba integration surfaced as implicit criteria on that call, but no structured requirements document or scoring rubric has been shared back to Zycus.

items.name: Congratulatory outreach + meeting ask
items.summary: Alexa Bradley congratulated Richa on being named to CPO Strategy Magazine's Future Procurement Champions Index and used it as a lead-in to request a 20-minute call next week to discuss a companion 2026 Procurement Roadmap; no deal-specific content, reply pending.

day_summary.overall: This was an outbound-only, relationship-building touch rather than deal progression — Alexa sent a congratulatory note to Richa on a procurement industry recognition, using it as a hook to request a 20-minute call next week tied to a 2026 Procurement Roadmap piece. It's a soft re-engagement play with no substantive deal content exchanged, and the ball is now in Richa's court to respond to the meeting ask.

cro_panel.intro: This is an early-stage, partner-adjacent tail-spend deal where the real obstacle is Apotex's own internal decision to consolidate spend with existing strategic partners before considering any new vendor; the recent breakthrough is that a Director-level buyer, Carla Baptista, showed up in person and engaged, but budget, NDA and executive sign-off are all still open

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

Unnamed alternative tail-spend providers were being evaluated in parallel as of the 07 Nov 2025 discovery call, with no update since. SAP Ariba is the incumbent S2P system of record but is background context, not an active competing proposal.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 5 is carried by differentiation strength +0.85 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'consolidat' in…; champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 25 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 25 (slipping backward): last genuine buyer touch 118d ago vs the ~30d cadence this stage needs — going quiet. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 9 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (5), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 25, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: differentiation strength +0.85 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'consolidat' in Next-Step/narrative

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c is 5.0 / AIS_Status__c Negative, reflecting real curiosity about Anna's agentic mechanics without budget or urgency behind it; consistent with the call evidence where Namish Shah asked detailed, specific questions about the autonomous negotiation agent but confirmed no formal project or budget.

business_case.evidence: Tail spend quantified at ~$100M/year with a target of 2-3% recurring savings, but the buyer has openly questioned whether that figure is competitive, and no buyer-owned ROI model exists yet.

items.detail: Leadership (VP Ryan Mitchell, Director Carla Baptista) paused vendor evaluations to consolidate tail spend with strategic partners; no Zycus engagement with decision-makers; no confirmation whether consolidation is complete or project has restarted. Buyer priority is strategic simplification over new-vendor adoption.

Target solution launch Jan 2026, savings Apr 2026 (new fiscal year) has passed without action. Consolidation pause issued 15 Apr with 6-week window (June restart target); now 70 days cooling with no buyer re-engagement. Close date 20 Aug 2026 (55 days out) is not credible without immediate restart.

No budget in place as of 7 Nov 2025; no formal project approved; no update on budget status post-consolidation pause. Deal is in exploration stage with no commercial commitment or approval path defined.

Single-threaded (Namish only, Manager level); zero access to VP/Director decision-makers who control budget, timing, and strategic direction. Namish has not brokered an introduction or escalated Zycus internally. Deal blocked at leadership level with no path to unblock.

Savings positioning (2–3% recurring) perceived as low by Namish vs 15–20% competitor claims; no tailored ROI model or written business case provided. Risk: lose on cost/value perception in a cost-driven tail-spend evaluation.

Competitor landscape unknown: Namish confirmed meeting multiple tail-spend solution providers but none named; no confirmation of formal RFP, shortlist, or bake-off process. Risk: lose to an unnamed competitor we are not tracking.

critical_signals.text: The 25 June 2026 Toronto roundtable brought a Director-level buyer, Carla Baptista, face to face with the deal for the first time, and both she and Namish Shah engaged closely and saw value in the wider platform.
The real threat isn't a rival tail-spend vendor, it's Apotex's own decision to consolidate spend with existing strategic partners before considering any new tool.
No budget has ever been confirmed for this project, and the NDA needed to size the deal and start a proof of concept still hasn't been signed.
Carla Baptista, Director of Global Procurement Excellence and Ryan Mitchell's direct report, is now personally engaged after being referenced only secondhand for months.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is real and recent (Toronto roundtable 25 Jun 2026) but has not translated into budget, NDA, or VP-level access after nine months.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Nov 2025, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 11 Nov 2025, and has sat in Qualified for roughly 240 days with no Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted submission date ever logged. Close date is 20 Aug 2026, 44 days from today (07 Jul 2026); at the current pace, with no budget confirmed, no NDA signed, and the internal consolidation review still unresolved as of 25 Jun 2026, that date is not credible.
evidence: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-04-16: Carla Baptista and Ryan Mitchell asked the team to consolidate tail spend before further evaluation, revisit in ~6 weeks (June).
Task 26 Jun 2026: Carla Baptista confirmed at the Toronto roundtable that 'nothing had changed much' on the consolidation push.
Avoma Intro Call 07 Nov 2025 (Namish Shah): 'No, currently we don't have a budget.'
north_star_verdict.headline: Director-level engagement just opened up (Carla Baptista, 25 Jun 2026) but the deal is still gated by Apotex's own tail-spend consolidation review, no confirmed budget, and an unsigned NDA, against a 20 Aug 2026 close date with no formal evaluation behind it.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pull out of near-term Pipeline; a realistic close depends on Apotex's own consolidation review concluding and a formal evaluation (budget, NDA, RFP) starting, plausibly Q4 2026 or later.

flags: SAVINGS POSITIONING EXPOSURE: Namish challenged Zycus's 2–3% savings pitch as low vs 15–20% competitor claims (7 Nov call). This is a win-probability risk in a cost-driven tail-spend eval. Reframe: lead with the 4.5–45% client outcome range (cited on the call but not anchored), tailor a written ROI model to Apotex's $100M tail-spend base ($2–4.5M annual savings at 2–4.5% conservative), and position the Tailwind partnership (IBM strategy + Zycus AI) as the premium-outcome path vs standalone-vendor claims.

Single-threaded at Manager level (Namish Shah) with zero access to the Economic Buyer (Ryan Mitchell, VP Global Procurement) or Carla Baptista (Director); the highest-leverage move is converting the Horizon Sept 2026 invite into a direct leadership touch.

Apotex is not comparing us to a rival vendor, it is comparing new-vendor spend to consolidating with existing strategic partners; the win-strategy is positioning ANA as automation that supports that consolidation, not a competing initiative.

No Apotex-specific ROI has been built; converting the generic 2-3% recurring savings benchmark into a number based on Apotex's ~$100M/year tail spend is the single biggest lever to re-open the evaluation.

Carla Baptista is now directly engaged after the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto roundtable; use this opening to get the NDA signed and secure a joint call with Ryan Mitchell (her manager) before the consolidation review drags on.

Position Anna as a low-friction complement to the ongoing strategic-partner consolidation, not a competing initiative, since Carla Baptista confirmed on 25 Jun 2026 that the consolidation effort itself has stalled internally.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal just got a real opening at the Director level; the priority now is converting that into a signed NDA and a VP-level connection before the consolidation review drags on further.

customer_preference.evidence: Namish Shah compared Zycus's 2-3% recurring savings guidance unfavorably against other providers' claims of 15-20% (Avoma Intro Call, 07 Nov 2025).

competitive_position.summary: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation remains the single strongest and most current threat, reconfirmed as recently as 25 Jun 2026 when Carla Baptista told the Zycus team 'nothing had changed much' on the internal push to consolidate tail spend with existing strategic partners. Unnamed alternative tail-spend providers were being evaluated in parallel as of the 07 Nov 2025 discovery call, with no update since. SAP Ariba is the incumbent S2P system of record but is background context, not an active competing proposal.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation
competitors.quote: Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further.
competitors.source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-04-16; reconfirmed Task 2026-06-26 ('nothing had changed much')
competitors.how_we_win: Position Anna as a low-friction complement that extracts savings from tail spend WHILE the consolidation effort continues, not as a competing new-vendor initiative; use Namish Shah and now Carla Baptista to carry that framing internally.

competitors.name: Unnamed tail-spend solution providers (actively comparing)
competitors.quote: We've met companies who have claimed 15–20%... we're still meeting a few solution providers just to understand what's out there.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic AI (autonomous negotiation agent Anna vs manual/copilot approaches), NPS (+40 vs peers), and pharma/healthcare customer proof (data integrity, SOC 2 / ISO 27001). Address the 2–3% savings perception (conservative estimate; cite 4.5–45% client outcomes and tailor ROI to Apotex's $100M tail-spend base).

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent S2P platform)
competitors.quote: No. We don't have Wax Digital. It's more SAP Ariba for the company.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the SAP Ariba/SAP-triggered integration path already scoped on the 07 Nov 2025 call so Anna is additive to, not a rip-and-replace of, the existing stack.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early-stage, partner-adjacent tail-spend deal where the real obstacle is Apotex's own internal decision to consolidate spend with existing strategic partners before considering any new vendor; the recent breakthrough is that a Director-level buyer, Carla Baptista, showed up in person and engaged, but budget, NDA and executive sign-off are all still open.

deal_risk.text: Ryan Mitchell, VP Global Procurement and the named approver of the consolidation decision, has never been directly engaged by Zycus.
The internal strategic-partner consolidation push that paused this evaluation in April 2026 was reconfirmed unresolved by Carla Baptista as recently as 25 Jun 2026.

win_position.text: Carla Baptista (Director, Global Procurement Excellence) engaged directly and asked detailed questions alongside Namish Shah at the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto roundtable, the first time a Director has shown up in person for this deal.

No budget has ever been confirmed and Apotex's own leadership paused new-vendor evaluation in April 2026 to consolidate tail spend with existing strategic partners, a review Carla Baptista confirmed on 25 Jun 2026 is still unresolved.
Namish Shah has openly compared Zycus's 2-3% savings guidance unfavorably to other providers claiming 15-20%, a live risk in a cost-driven tail-spend evaluation.

deal_momentum.text: After a quiet consolidation-pause period, the deal owner re-engaged Namish Shah directly by email on 09 Mar 2026 and secured a reply, then met both Namish Shah and Carla Baptista in person at a Toronto roundtable on 25 Jun 2026.
The stage has not moved past Qualified since 11 Nov 2025 (about eight months), and the 20 Aug 2026 close date has no formal evaluation, budget, or signed NDA behind it.

customer_commitment.text: Namish Shah has stayed engaged across nine months of email exchanges, brought Carla Baptista to a Zycus-adjacent event, and both reviewed detailed product questions on Anna's mechanics and integration.
Neither Namish Shah nor Carla Baptista has signed the NDA/CDA that Zycus has offered since 07 Nov 2025, which is blocking formal proof-of-concept scoping.

hard.ais_why:
• On the Nov 2025 call Namish Shah asked 'Have you tried using or integrating to any AI copilots yet?' and probed specifically 'if you only focus on tailspin, would it be Anna?' showing he was tracking the Anna autonomous negotiation pitch.
• Apotex is in exploration phase with no formal budget and timeline targeting Jan launch for Apr 2026 savings. SF Next Step notes the team is consolidating tail spend first before any vendor decision.
• Conversation paused June 2026 pending consolidation progress. Real engagement on Anna and agentic tail-spend savings, but no champion, no budget, no urgency - upper-curious-low edge.
hard.next_step:
5/4 - checked in with team, still working through consolidation efforts.  Invitations to Horizon Sept event have been extended.

4/15 - spent time with Namish Shah in person at NAPES.  Interest in ANA but Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further.  Tail spend is ~$100Mn/year. Conversations to resume in 6 weeks (June) when they know better how progress is coming.

RESTART URGENCY: Planned June 2026 restart (from 15 Apr '6 weeks' window) has not occurred; 70 days cooling since last buyer engagement (17 Apr). Consolidation status unknown. Single highest-leverage move: re-engage Namish immediately to learn whether consolidation is complete, map leadership green-light status, and secure a joint session with the VP (Ryan Mitchell) and Director (Carla Baptista) to reframe ANA as complementary to (not competitive with) the strategic-partner strategy.

SINGLE-THREADED RISK: Only one buyer contact (Namish Shah, Manager level) engaged across 7+ months; zero contact roles on the opportunity; zero access to the VP (Ryan Mitchell) or Director (Carla Baptista) who paused the evaluation and control budget/timing. Deal cannot advance without multi-threading to decision-maker level. Add buyer-side contact roles (Namish, plus VP/Director when accessible) and secure a joint executive session post-consolidation to validate business case and timing.

deltas.subject: SAVINGS POSITIONING EXPOSURE: Namish challenged Zycus's 2–3% savings pitch as low vs 15–20% competitor claims (7 Nov call). This is a win-probability risk in a cost-driven tail-spend eval. Reframe: lead with the 4.5–45% client outcome range (cited on the call but not anchored), tailor a written ROI model to Apotex's $100M tail-spend base ($2–4.5M annual savings at 2–4.5% conservative), and position the Tailwind partnership (IBM strategy + Zycus AI) as the premium-outcome path vs standalone-vendor claims.

value.name: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation
value.quote: Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further.
value.source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-04-16; reconfirmed Task 2026-06-26 ('nothing had changed much')
value.how_we_win: Position Anna as a low-friction complement that extracts savings from tail spend WHILE the consolidation effort continues, not as a competing new-vendor initiative; use Namish Shah and now Carla Baptista to carry that framing internally.

value.name: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation
value.quote: Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further.
value.how_we_win: Reposition ANA as automation that supports and accelerates the strategic-partner consolidation Apotex is already pursuing, rather than as a competing initiative requiring a separate vendor decision.

value.name: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation
value.quote: Carla Baptista (Director - Global Procurement Excellence) and Ryan Mitchell (head of procurement) have asked the team to try and consolidate tail spend and push more business to strategic partners in advance of evaluating further. Conversations to resume in 6 weeks (June).
value.how_we_win: Position ANA as complementary to strategic-partner strategy: autonomous negotiation WITHIN the partner ecosystem (amplifies leverage, visibility, savings) without adding vendor count or disrupting consolidation. Reframe from 'new vendor evaluation' to 'partner-enablement tool' and secure VP/Director buy-in post-consolidation.

packets.subject: Do Nothing / Strategic Partner Consolidation

value.name: Unnamed tail-spend solution providers (actively comparing)
value.quote: We've met companies who have claimed 15–20%... we're still meeting a few solution providers just to understand what's out there.
value.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic AI (autonomous negotiation agent Anna vs manual/copilot approaches), NPS (+40 vs peers), and pharma/healthcare customer proof (data integrity, SOC 2 / ISO 27001). Address the 2–3% savings perception (conservative estimate; cite 4.5–45% client outcomes and tailor ROI to Apotex's $100M tail-spend base).

packets.subject: Unnamed tail-spend solution providers (actively comparing)

value.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent S2P platform)
value.quote: No. We don't have Wax Digital. It's more SAP Ariba for the company.
value.how_we_win: Lead with the SAP Ariba/SAP-triggered integration path already scoped on the 07 Nov 2025 call so Anna is additive to, not a rip-and-replace of, the existing stack.

value.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent S2P platform)
value.quote: ...a person would put a put in a requisition in SAP, and the...
value.source: Avoma intro call transcript, Namish Shah, 07 Nov 2025
value.how_we_win: Position ANA/Agentic AI as a complement that sits on top of the existing SAP requisitioning workflow rather than a rip-and-replace, lowering the perceived switching cost.

value.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent S2P platform)
value.quote: You have a mix of SAP Ariba… limited AI embedded in their system just yet.
value.how_we_win: Ariba is incumbent for S2P but Namish confirmed exploring tail-spend solutions (implying Ariba does not meet the need today). Wedge: Zycus agentic AI (Anna) vs Ariba's manual/limited-AI tail-spend module; organically-built single codebase vs bolt-on acquisitions; NPS +40 vs Ariba negative. Position ANA as a complementary tail-spend layer on top of Ariba (integration-friendly), not a rip-and-replace.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba (incumbent S2P platform)
